description:
You are a capacity-planner who has just been given access to a fresh Linux machine (your interactive shell starts in /home/user).
  Set up a *very small* capacity-planning workspace and store one initial resource-usage snapshot so that it can be inspected later by an automated tool.

  Required end-state of the system
  --------------------------------
  1. A directory named “capacity_planning” must exist directly under /home/user  
     • Absolute path: /home/user/capacity_planning  

  2. Inside that directory there must be one log file that captures the current CPU load, memory usage, and root-filesystem usage.  
     • Absolute path: /home/user/capacity_planning/snapshot_001.log  

     The file must contain **exactly three section-header lines** (the automated grader will look for *those* literal marker lines, nothing else):  
       === CPU_LOAD ===  
       === MEMORY_USAGE ===  
       === DISK_ROOT ===  

     The command outputs should be placed **immediately after** their respective marker lines (one or more lines each), but the grader will only verify that the marker lines themselves are present and in that exact order.  
       • Under “CPU_LOAD” put the output of the shell command normally used to inspect load average.  
       • Under “MEMORY_USAGE” put the output of the shell command that reports memory usage in megabytes.  
       • Under “DISK_ROOT” put the output of the shell command that reports human-readable disk usage for “/”.  

     Example of the exact skeleton that the grader will look for (the actual numeric values may differ):  
       === CPU_LOAD ===  
       20:33:10 up  3:00,  1 user,  load average: 0.05, 0.02, 0.00  
       === MEMORY_USAGE ===  
                     total        used        free      shared  buff/cache   available  
       Mem:           3900         800         500          70        2600        2800  
       Swap:             0           0           0  
       === DISK_ROOT ===  
       Filesystem     Type  Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on  
       /dev/sda1      ext4   30G  6.0G   22G  22% /  

     The automated tests check only for:  
       • existence of /home/user/capacity_planning/snapshot_001.log  
       • presence of the three marker lines *in the above order*.  
       The exact command output lines that follow each marker are **not** graded, so you do not have to worry about their runtime values.

  3. Still inside /home/user/capacity_planning, create a tiny status file so that a supervisor script can quickly confirm the snapshot was produced.  
     • Absolute path: /home/user/capacity_planning/verification.log  
     • The file must contain **one single line** (no extra spaces, no extra blank lines):  
       snapshot_001.log created  

  Permissions & constraints
  -------------------------
  • All files must be owned by the current unprivileged user (you do not have sudo).  
  • The directory and both files must be readable by any user (mode 644 for files, 755 for the directory is sufficient).  
  • Do not place the workspace anywhere outside /home/user.  
  • Only standard GNU/Linux user-land tools found in a minimal distribution should be needed (mkdir, uptime, free, df, etc.).  
  • The whole task can be accomplished with roughly 3-4 shell commands.

  Deliverable summary for the grader
  ----------------------------------
  After you are finished, the grader will verify:

  1. Directory exists:      /home/user/capacity_planning  
  2. Log file exists:       /home/user/capacity_planning/snapshot_001.log  
  3. Snapshot log includes, in order:  
       line 1: === CPU_LOAD ===  
       somewhere after: === MEMORY_USAGE ===  
       somewhere after: === DISK_ROOT ===  
  4. Verification file exists with *exact* content “snapshot_001.log created”.

  Provide no additional output files or directories.
truth:
Expected artefacts and their minimal required contents:

  1. Directory:
     Path: /home/user/capacity_planning
     Must exist.

  2. Snapshot file:
     Path: /home/user/capacity_planning/snapshot_001.log
     Must contain at least the following three lines *in this exact order*:
       === CPU_LOAD ===
       === MEMORY_USAGE ===
       === DISK_ROOT ===
     (Other lines can appear between them; that is acceptable.)

  3. Verification file:
     Path: /home/user/capacity_planning/verification.log
     Content must be exactly:
       snapshot_001.log created
     (No leading/trailing whitespace, no extra lines.)
